data.title: MDA Space announces offering of C$250M senior unsecured notes due 2030
data.description:
• MDA Space (MDA:CA) on Thursday said it has priced a private placement offering and entered into an underwriting agreement to sell, pursuant to the offering, C$250 million aggregate principal amount of 7.00% senior unsecured notes due 2030.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about December 23, 2025.
  • The notes will be issued at a price of C$1,000 per C$1,000 principal amount of notes. 
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds of the offering to refinance existing outstanding indebtedness under the Credit Agreement.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Mercado Libre issues $750M of 2033 senior unsecured notes
data.description:
• Mercado Libre (MELI) on Thursday announced the issuance of its 2033 senior unsecured notes for a total amount of $750 million.
  • Transaction Highlights
    • Title of Securities: 4.900% Notes due 2033
    • Security Type: Senior Unsecured Notes
    • Format: SEC Registered
    • Issuer Rating: BBB- (S&P) / BBB- (Fitch)
    • Size: USD 750 million
    • Tenor: 7 years (expiring in January 2033)
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: CDC vaccine panel voting on hepatitis b shots postponed over confusion
data.description:
An influential vaccine advisory panel to the CDC delayed voting on questions regarding if and when to administer the hepatitis b shot to newborns amid confusion over the language of the questions themselves.

Although the voting questions were posted on Wednesday, some Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices members complained that the language of the voting questions had been changed several times.

"This is the third version of the questions that most of the ACIP members received in 72 hours," said Joseph Hibbeln, the former acting chief of the Section of Nutritional Neurosciences at the National Institute on Alcohol Abuse and Alcoholism and a psychiatrist. "We don’t even have a slide explicitly describing what questions we're supposed to answer."

ACIP members ended up voting 7-3 to delay voting until Friday.

The first question, as published by ACIP on Wednesday, stated that the offspring of mothers who test negative for the virus should discuss with their health care provider about whether their child should get the vaccine, a process known as shared decision-making. Mothers who decide to skip the birth dose should wait at least two months should they decide to have their child receive the series of shots later on. 

The question also recommends that children of mothers who test positive for hepatitis b get the vaccine at birth.

A second question stated that in cases where a mother's hepatitis b status is unknown, the child should receive the shot. An earlier version had stated that the decision in these cases should be made in consultation with a doctor. 

The third question asks whether serology testing should be offered  -- and covered by insurance -- before subsequent vaccine dose administration. The hepatitis b vaccine is usually given across three doses by the time a child is 18 months old.  

The delay is the second for the vaccine after voting was tabled at the group's previous meeting in September. 

The three hepatitis b vaccines approved in the US are: Merck's (MRK) Recombivax HB; GSK's (GSK) Engerix-B; and Dynavax Technologies' (DVAX) Heplisav-B.

data.title: SentinelOne CFO Barbara Larson to retire, insider Barry Padgett to serve as interim CFO
data.description:
• SentinelOne (S) announced that Barbara Larson notified the company of her intention to resign from her position as the CFO and principal financial officer at a future date that is mutually agreeable to both the company and Larson (currently expected in mid-January 2026). 
  • Effective as of the Larson's resignation date, Barry Padgett, the company’s current Chief Growth Officer, will serve as the interim CFO and principal financial officer, the company said.
  • Padgett has served as the company’s Chief Growth Officer since March 2025. Prior to joining the company, he served as the CEO of Amperity, a customer cloud platform, from March 2022 to June 2024 and President and COO at Amperity from April 2020 to February 2022. 
  • The company has also appointed Mark Barrenechea to serve as a member of the Board. Barrenechea will serve as a Class II director.
  • Barrenechea served as the CEO and Chief Technology Officer of OpenText from January 2012 to August 2025. 
  • Filing

data.title: EQB expects ROE to approach 12% in 2026 while advancing strategic PC Financial integration
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: EQB Inc. (EQB:CA) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Chadwick Westlake, CEO, described fiscal 2025 as a difficult year of significant change but highlighted accomplishments including 10% year-over-year loan growth, 36% year-over-year growth in off-balance sheet CMHC insured multi-unit residential mortgages, 18% growth in EQ Bank customers, and the October launch of a small business banking offering that already achieved $140 million in business deposits by month-end.
  • Westlake emphasized a renewed focus on efficiency, stating "we would complete our product shelf and move back to our industry-leading ROE profile, even with the competitive disadvantage of standardized capital treatment." He also noted, "our de-cumulation business... increased 36% last year and remains poised to continue delivering double-digit growth."
  • The CEO reported progress with net interest margin, stating "the outcome for Q4 was progress with NIM expanding 4 basis points sequentially to 2.01%."
  • EQB executed its first-ever restructuring, resulting in a pretax charge, with Westlake noting, "the benefit is not in our Q4 results as it was executed at the end of October, but it will become evident in Q1 results."
  • On strategic direction, Westlake stated, "our PC Financial acquisition and Loblaw partnership are going to be game changers," and highlighted a focus on becoming "the largest Challenger in Canada."
  • Anilisa Sainani, CFO, stated, "diluted EPS for the fourth quarter was $1.53, and ROE was 7.5%."
  • Sainani explained, "we announced an increase in the quarterly dividend to $0.57 per share, up from $0.55 last quarter and $0.49 last year, as we continue our strong track record of dividend increases."
  • Sainani also detailed, "we recognized a final pretax charge of $92 million" for restructuring and projected, "we expect approximately $45 million in annual expense savings in fiscal 2026."
  • Marlene Lenarduzzi, Chief Risk Officer, reported, "PCLs were $7.8 million in personal, $11.8 million in commercial and $0.2 million in equipment financing," and stated, "our ACL rate increased to 41 basis points, up 8 basis points sequentially and 9 basis points year-over-year."

Outlook

  • Westlake reaffirmed medium-term objectives and projected, "for 2026, our outlook excludes the impact of PC Financial. And I would expect ROE to improve materially from the 7.5% we reported for Q4. What that looks like is highly dependent on the macroeconomic backdrop, but we feel, based on our estimates today, that could look something like approaching 12%, increasing even higher later in fiscal 2026."
  • Sainani provided, "we expect total expense growth to be in the low single digits next year, inclusive of the restructuring program savings. We also expect to deliver positive operating leverage with a low 50s efficiency ratio, with additional upside as the revenue environment picks up."
  • The company expects loan under management growth in the high single digits to low double digits in 2026.
  • Net interest margins are anticipated to "remain around the 2% plus level."

Financial Results

  • Loan under management increased 10% year-over-year and 1% sequentially to $74.5 billion.
  • Deposits rose 9% year-over-year and 1% sequentially to $36.1 billion, with EQ Bank's demand deposits growing 38% year-over-year and 3% sequentially.
  • Net interest income was $265 million for Q4, down 2% year-over-year and up 1% versus last quarter.
  • Noninterest revenue of $43.5 million was down 15% from last year and 9% from last quarter.
  • Noninterest expenses increased by 11% compared to last year, attributed to growth-related investments and higher premises costs.
  • The bank's CET1 ratio was flat at 13.3%.

Q&A

  • John Aiken, Jefferies, asked about resolution timelines for commercial loan impairments. Lenarduzzi answered, "it's approximately taking anywhere between 12 to 18 months."
  • On strategic composition of commercial portfolios, Westlake stated, "over 80% is insured and our priority has been the CMHC insured multi-unit as our top focus and being a #1 alternative CRE lender."
  • Gabriel Dechaine, National Bank Financial, inquired about the outlook for residential mortgage impairments. Lenarduzzi replied, "early-stage delinquency in that portfolio has been moderating, and you can see that decline over the year... towards the end of the second half, we'll see those gross impaireds come down."
  • Dechaine questioned expense savings from restructuring; Sainani confirmed, "you would have already seen the discipline come through this quarter, but... that $45 million I was talking about... will play through 2026."
  • Mehmed Rizvanovic, Scotiabank, asked if the 41 basis point ACL rate is a new normal. Lenarduzzi responded, "if the credit environment improves, for sure, we would see that coverage... come down."
  • Darko Mihelic, RBC, asked about causes of residential mortgage impairments. Lenarduzzi explained, "some of it is related to job loss, some of it is just customers we're holding on for a long time and having a harder time as the macroeconomic environment shifts."
  • Etienne Ricard, BMO, asked about growth potential for credit cards versus mortgages. Westlake replied, "don't just think about this as a credit card. I think about this very much as payment solutions for Canadians... backed by the power of the PC Optimum program."
  • Doug Young, Desjardins, asked about the path to 12%+ ROE. Westlake stated, "step one is the expense side that analysts have spoken about that we've delivered on... as the revenue environment picks up, that will improve, especially with the net interest margin discipline."
  • Stephen Boland, Raymond James, asked about the pullback from insured single-family loans. Westlake replied, "insured is a thinner margin. It's very competitive, and we want to focus our capitals as well where we can win."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a neutral to slightly cautious tone, pressing for specifics around credit loss trends, expense management, and the impact of strategic initiatives, with recurring follow-ups on credit quality and return metrics.
  • Management’s sentiment was more confident in prepared remarks, emphasizing opportunities for growth and efficiency gains, but was more measured and occasionally defensive in Q&A, particularly around credit and expense trajectory. Westlake used phrases such as "I want to be clear" and "I've always believed our goal should be to focus on doing a few big things well."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management displayed increased specificity on expense actions and forward targets, while analysts’ skepticism about credit quality and capital deployment persisted but did not intensify.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance shifted to a more specific outlook for 2026, with an explicit ROE target approaching 12% and expectations of positive operating leverage.
  • Strategic focus sharpened with the announced integration of PC Financial, product shelf completion, and enhanced digital capabilities, compared to the previous quarter's broader statements about returning to growth.
  • Analysts continued to focus on credit trends, cost discipline, and strategic execution, but current management responses provided greater clarity on restructuring and capital allocation.
  • Key metrics such as diluted EPS and ROE declined compared to Q3, largely due to higher provisions and restructuring charges, while deposit and loan growth remained resilient.
  • Management’s tone shifted from initial strategic priority setting in Q3 to detailed actions and specific forward-looking financial targets in Q4.

Risks and Concerns

  • Macroeconomic uncertainty remains a principal concern, with elevated provisions for credit losses and longer resolution timelines for impaired loans highlighted by management.
  • Lenarduzzi cautioned that "this outlook is highly dependent on macroeconomic conditions and based on the expectations that we will avoid a deep recessionary scenario."
  • Increased allowances for credit losses and rising gross impaired loans, particularly in specific regional and vintage residential mortgages, were identified as ongoing risks.
  • Management outlined mitigation through disciplined cost controls, portfolio adjustments, and continued focus on risk management and provisioning.

Final Takeaway

EQB Inc. closed a challenging year with decisive strategic actions, including restructuring and targeted investments to streamline operations, and is positioned for improved profitability in 2026. The company expects material improvement in ROE, positive operating leverage, and continued capital strength, while focusing on executing the PC Financial integration and driving growth in key lending and deposit businesses. Management remains vigilant on credit quality and macroeconomic headwinds but anticipates gradual improvement in asset quality and expense efficiency as core initiatives take hold.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: ServiceTitan edges up after delivering solid Q3 results
data.description:
ServiceTitan (TTAN) shares edged up 3% during early post-market trading on Thursday after the company delivered its third-quarter fiscal 2026 financial results.

For the quarter ended October 31, ServiceTitan, which offers a software platform for the trades industry, reported adjusted earnings per share of $0.24, which was more than the consensus estimate of $0.15. Its GAAP EPS was ($0.42) compared to the ($0.45) estimate.

Revenue for the third quarter was $249.2M, which was more than the $238.3M estimate.

Looking ahead, ServiceTitan expects fourth-quarter revenue to range from $244M to $246M and full-year revenue to range from $951M to $953M.

"I am deeply humbled by how much progress we've made during our first year as a public company," said Ara Mahdessian, ServiceTitan's co-founder and CEO. "While we've come a long way together, I have never been more confident that our opportunity to build the operating system for the trades is only just beginning."

data.title: Dollar General's back to basics focus is paying off -- analysts
data.description:
Value-seeking consumers provided Dollar General (DG) with a strong third quarter and upbeat outlook for the year with broad-based gains across merchandise categories and increased traffic boosting the company’s sales by 4.6% and profits by 44%.

“Dollar General’s back to basics focus is paying off, with comps that are hovering around industry growth of 2.5% to 3%. Key from here will be striking the right balance between much anticipated margin recovery and reinvestment needs for wage rate, price positioning, and supply chain enhancements to sustain momentum in a competitive food retail industry,” Evercore ISI analyst Michael Montani said in a note to clients.

The improved outlook for the year reflected expectations for a strong holiday season and market share gains, amplified by the company’s further expansion into perishable food and increased presence of higher income shoppers.

Comparable sales, however, remain the sticking point, Wells Fargo’s Edward Kelly says.

“While the complexion of the comp was better, 2.5% same store sales still doesn’t inspire given the likely higher long-term leverage point,” Kelly says, holding onto an Equal Weight rating and $115 price target given “lackluster comps and competitive issues.”

For Gordon Haskett’s Chuck Grom, traffic growth is indeed a key barometer of a retailer’s health. But here, Dollar General’s (DG) sustained performance in this metric stands out, especially against peer Dollar Tree (DLTR).

Additionally, Dollar General (DG) noted that average unit retail was up while units per transaction was down within the basket.

“These shopping patterns are consistent with what the company has seen historically when consumers are under pressure,” Grom says, encouraged that the company continues to drive strong results despite macro pressure, upgrading Dollar General (DG) to Buy with a $140 price target.

Dollar General (DG) ended the day with a gain of 14% making it the best performing stock within the S&P 500.

data.title: Chevron flies Venezuela staff to oil sites despite U.S. warning - Bloomberg
data.description:
Chevron (CVX) is flying employees from Caracas to its oil production sites in Venezuela despite a warning from the U.S. Federal Aviation Administration that air travel in the region may be unsafe due to military satellite interference, Bloomberg reported Thursday.

Chevron (CVX) - the only U.S. oil producer still operating in Venezuela - reportedly sent employees Monday to its facilities around the country, part of the company's routine weekly oversight of oil ventures that pump ~25% of Venezuela’s crude output.

"Chevron's operations in Venezuela continue in full compliance with laws and regulations applicable to its business, as well as the sanctions frameworks provided for by the U.S. government," a company spokesperson said.

The safety of Caribbean airspace has become an issue as the Trump administration continues its military buildup in the region, and coincides with a surge in communication jamming that has affected satellite navigation and other systems in the area, making air travel risky, although most domestic commercial and private flights have maintained operations under threat from the Maduro government.

The Trump administration has barred most U.S. companies from doing business in Venezuela, but it allows Chevron (CVX) to maintain minimal maintenance on equipment in its joint ventures and to keep exporting Venezuelan oil to the U.S.

data.title: IDEX Corp names new CFO
data.description:
• IDEX (IEX) appointed Sean M. Gillen as senior vice president and chief financial officer, effective January 5, 2026.
  • Interim CFO Akhil Mahendra will return to his prior role as vice president of Corporate Development.
  • Gillen brings nearly seven years of CFO experience from AAR CORP. (AIR), along with prior roles at USG Corporation and Goldman Sachs.

data.title: Cooper Cos. jumps after starting strategic review, naming new board chair
data.description:
Cooper Cos. (COO) soared 12% in after hours trading after initiating a strategic review of its businesses and naming a new board chair.

Cooper (COO) is undergoing a formal and comprehensive strategic review of ts businesses, corporate structure, strategy, operations, and capital allocation priorities, according to a statement on Thursday. The company will look at options including  partnerships, joint ventures, divestitures, mergers, business combinations, and other transactions.

The company named Colleen Jay as chairman to succeed Robert Weiss, effective Jan. 2. 

The business review comes after it was reported in October that activist investor Jana Partners took a stake and planned to push the company to review its strategic alternatives. Jana wants Cooper to combine its contact-lens unit with rival Bausch + Lomb (BLCO), the WSJ reported. 

During its strategic review, the company will focus on repurchasing shares under its recently announced $2 billion share repurchase program.

Cooper Cos. (COO) also reported Q3 results on Thursday.
